exercises.title: B: Open Cloze (A prisoner's view)
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.passage:
Although I dislike (9) ...... locked in this place, I know I made some terrible choices. If I hadn't broken the law, I wouldn't (10) ...... ended up here. I really (11) ...... I had listened to my family. But nobody can change the past, (12) ...... they?

My room is extremely small – it is hardly bigger (13) ...... a bathroom! It is (14) ...... narrow that two people can't stand side by side. Both (15) ...... us try to keep it tidy, which helps.

My sister lives far away, but she writes (16) ...... me every Sunday. Her letters make prison life easier.

If I leave this year, I (17) ...... never get into trouble again. Freedom will be (18) ...... an amazing gift!

exercises.title: C: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
Emma was five when she first learned to swim. **age**
Emma first learned to swim ...... five.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: "at the age of" is the only form that completes the sentence correctly.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "at the age of" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "Emma was five when she first learned to swim. age".

explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "set up" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "Why don't you start a school magazine? set".

explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "get over" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "How long did it take him to recover from the flu? over".

exercises.title: D: Word Formation (A new painter)
exercises.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.
exercises.passage:
Were you (29) ...... enough to see the opening night at the Riverside Gallery? I was, and I enjoyed the new (30) ...... of Sofia Martin's paintings. Sofia is not (31) ...... yet, but she is clearly an (32) ...... with a lot of talent.

Her paintings, without (33) ......, are original and (34) ...... . She often paints places of great (35) ...... beauty, like mountains and rivers, but she makes them look bright and (36) ...... . Many of her works are about (37) ...... and hope. It is difficult to become a (38) ...... artist, but Sofia has an excellent chance.

exercises.title: E: Prepositions
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Don't criticise him ...... making one small mistake.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: "for" is the only form that completes the sentence correctly.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "for" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "Don't criticise him ...... making one small mistake.".

items.sentence: I'm fed up ...... hearing the same excuse every day.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: "with" is the only form that completes the sentence correctly.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.
